evidence_quotes.quote: gaming companies can make sure that games we've paid for stop working.
It doesn't matter if we bought a digital copy or a physical copy.
If I buy a game, I want to be able to play it from today, tomorrow, and for years to come, especially if I bought a physical copy.
How can it be okay that they can remove something I/we have paid for!?

paragraphs.text: Hello! I'm here to tell you that we need your help to improve/save the gaming industry. As it stands now, gaming companies can make sure that games we've paid for stop working. It doesn't matter if we bought a digital copy or a physical copy. How can it be okay that they can remove something I/we have paid for!? Even if it's a game that's made to be played online, it's possible to make sure that you can play it alone with bots. It's not the hardest thing to do, but the people in charge of a gaming company don't want to spend money on making sure that a developer develops it. If I buy a game, I want to be able to play it from today, tomorrow, and for years to come, especially if I bought a physical copy. Please help us and thank you for listening to me!
